message:
fix(Cargo.toml): remove local-path frankensqlite patches so `cargo install --git` works; add contributor-template for sibling-checkout dev (issue #250 bug 1)

`Cargo.toml` previously carried a `[patch.crates-io]` table that pointed
every `fsqlite*` crate at a sibling `../frankensqlite/crates/fsqlite*`
path. That let core contributors hack on frankensqlite alongside
beads_rust without relocking, but it broke the single most common
install path for external users:

    cargo install --git https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/beads_rust.git

That install clones the repo into `~/.cargo/git/checkouts/...` and runs
`cargo install` from there. The sibling `../frankensqlite` directory
does not exist in that layout, so every `fsqlite*` dependency resolves
to a path that isn't there, and cargo hard-errors before even starting
the build. Issue #250 bug 1 was exactly this failure mode, reported
anywhere from fresh CI containers to manual installs on Apple Silicon.

Fix: delete the path-based patches. `fsqlite` 0.1.2+ and every
`fsqlite-*` transitive crate are published on crates.io now, so the
default `[dependencies]` resolution works without the patches. The one
exception is `fastmcp-rust`, which is still unpublished and must stay
on its git patch — that patch references a git URL, not a local path,
so it is portable and is kept.

For core contributors who want the sibling-checkout workflow back, we
ship a ready-to-copy template at `scripts/dev-local-frankensqlite.toml`
plus inline instructions. The template lives in `scripts/` (not
`.cargo/`) so it does not accidentally get vendored into the published
crate by `cargo package`. The commented-out NOTE in `Cargo.toml` points
contributors at it.

Also documents the reasoning inline in `Cargo.toml` so the next
maintainer does not regress this — the patches would otherwise look
like an obvious performance win to re-add.

No behavioral change for callers who already had working installs.
Fresh `cargo install --git` invocations now succeed; sibling-checkout
dev still works by copying the template to `.cargo/config.toml`, which
the companion gitignore change (if landed) keeps in-tree.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
